CLAUDE.md — Edward Kao Personal Website

Project Overview

Personal academic/portfolio website for Edward Kao, hosted on GitHub Pages at https://edwardkao6413.github.io. Built with Jekyll (pinned to 3.9.x via github-pages gem), plain CSS with design tokens, and vanilla JS.

Owner

  • Name: Edward Kao
  • Role: Data Scientist & Bioinformatics Researcher
  • Email: edwardkao6413@gmail.com
  • GitHub: https://github.com/edwardkao6413

Tech Stack

  • Static site generator: Jekyll 3.9.x (via github-pages gem — do NOT upgrade to Jekyll 4.x)
  • Templating: Liquid
  • Styling: Plain CSS with CSS custom properties (no Tailwind, no Bootstrap, no external CSS framework)
  • JavaScript: Vanilla JS only (no React, no jQuery)
  • Hosting: GitHub Pages, branch main, root /
  • Icons: Font Awesome 6.5.0 via CDN

Repository Structure

/
├── CLAUDE.md                # This file
├── _config.yml              # Jekyll site-wide config
├── Gemfile                  # Ruby gems (github-pages + webrick)
├── index.html               # Homepage
├── publications.md          # Publications sub-page
├── career.md                # Career sub-page
├── other-works.md           # Other Works sub-page
├── _layouts/
│   └── default.html         # Shared HTML shell
├── _includes/
│   ├── navbar.html          # Fixed top navbar (hamburger on mobile)
│   └── footer.html          # Footer with social icons and copyright
├── assets/
│   ├── css/main.css         # All styles (design tokens + component styles)
│   ├── js/main.js           # Navbar toggle + keyword typewriter animation
│   └── img/                 # Images (profile.jpg goes here when ready)
├── doc/
│   └── CV_Edward.pdf        # Resume PDF
├── references/
│   └── web-structure.md     # Original site spec/template reference
├── docs/superpowers/plans/  # Implementation plans
├── .dev-manager/            # Dev-manager workflow state and plans
└── .agents/                 # Local agent skills (e.g. UI-Design-Master)

Active Agents

  • UI-Design-Master — .agents/UI-Design-Master/SKILL — use for ALL styling, CSS, and visual design decisions. Every visual change (CSS, layout, SVG styling, typography, colour, spacing) MUST be passed through this agent before implementation. Always invoke before writing or updating CSS.
  • dev-manager — .dev-manager/workflow.md — orchestrates planning, implementation, and review stages.

Design Direction (UI-Design-Master: Editorial Luxury / Refined Restraint)

  • Aesthetic: Quietly confident academic portfolio — feels like a curated research monograph, not a generic dev site
  • Tone: Editorial luxury / refined restraint
  • Differentiation: Deep navy + warm cream palette with serif italic display font

Color Tokens

| Token | Value | Use | |—|—|—| | --color-navy-900 | #0d1b2a | Navbar background, headings | | --color-navy-700 | #1b3a52 | Hover states, borders | | --color-teal-600 | #1a7a6e | Primary accent — links, highlights, cursor | | --color-teal-400 | #2aa898 | Hover on accent elements | | --color-cream-50 | #faf8f4 | Page background | | --color-neutral-900 | #2c2c2c | Body text |

Typography

  • Display font: Cormorant Garamond (Google Fonts) — italic, weight 400/600
  • Body font: DM Sans (Google Fonts) — weight 400/500/600
  • Never use: Inter, Roboto, Arial as display; purple gradients; flat solid backgrounds

Motion

  • Hero entrance: fadeUp animation (0.8s, staggered per element)
  • One high-impact moment only — no scattered micro-animations
  • prefers-reduced-motion respected (all durations set to 0.01ms)

Accessibility

  • WCAG AA: 4.5:1 contrast normal text, 3:1 large text
  • Touch targets ≥ 44×44px on mobile
  • focus-visible outlines on all interactive elements (teal, 2px, offset 4px)
  • prefers-reduced-motion media query included

Navigation Pages

Each sub-page is a Jekyll Markdown file (publications.md, career.md, other-works.md). Jekyll compiles them into routes using the permalink front matter key and renders them through the default.html layout, so they inherit the navbar, footer, fonts, and CSS automatically. Edit the .md file to add content; GitHub Pages rebuilds on push.

Label Route Source file Status
Resume /doc/CV_Edward.pdf doc/CV_Edward.pdf PDF (replace placeholder when ready)
Publications /publications publications.md Stub — edit to add content
Career /career career.md Active — SVG timeline with month-precise bars
Other Works /other-works other-works.md Stub — edit to add content

Key Config Values (_config.yml)

  • baseurl: "" — do not change; changing it breaks all | relative_url links
  • navbar.resume_url: /doc/CV_Edward.pdf — update when real PDF is placed
  • social.github: https://github.com/edwardkao6413

Development Commands

# Install gems (first time only)
bundle install

# Local preview
bundle exec jekyll serve
# → http://127.0.0.1:4000

# Build static output (for checking _site/)
bundle exec jekyll build

Important Constraints

  1. Jekyll version: The github-pages gem locks Jekyll to 3.9.x. Do not add Jekyll 4-only features (e.g. webrick is required explicitly because of Ruby 3.x compatibility).
  2. No JS frameworks: Vanilla JS only. No npm, no node_modules, no build tools.
  3. CSS architecture: All styles in assets/css/main.css. Use CSS custom properties (--var) for all tokens — no hardcoded colours or sizes outside :root.
  4. Exclude from build: Gemfile, Gemfile.lock, docs/, .dev-manager/, .agents/, references/, README.md are all in _config.yml exclude list — do not remove them.
  5. Resume PDF: Do not use touch doc/CV_Edward.pdf — it creates a zero-byte file that renders as a broken PDF. Either place a real PDF or use a disabled navbar link.
  6. Visual changes: Every change to CSS, layout, SVG styling, colour, spacing, or typography MUST be reviewed by the UI-Design-Master agent before implementation. No exceptions.

Career Timeline Architecture

The career page (career.md) uses a month-precise SVG timeline:

  • Data: _data/career.yml — each entry has start_year, start_month, end_year, end_month (integers 1–12), plus optional present: true for ongoing roles.
  • Math: months_from_top(year, month) = (display_end_year − year) × 12 − (month − 1). Always multiply before dividing (months × 80 / 12) to avoid Liquid integer truncation.
  • present: true: Sets bar_top = svg_top_pad directly, pinning the bar to the NOW dot. Do not compute bar_top via the month formula for present events.
  • Labels: Rendered as SVG <text> elements inside the viewBox (not HTML overlays), so they scale correctly at all viewport widths.
  • start_year / start_month: Declared at the top of career.md. If the earliest event changes, update both variables so svg_height and the spine extend far enough.
  • Two label columns: Education + Research labels go to the left column (label_left_x = 136); Work + Amateur Interest labels go to the right column (label_right_x = 290). This structurally prevents most cross-category overlaps.
  • leader_y_offset: Integer px nudge from the bar midpoint (0 = exact midpoint of bar; positive = push down; negative = push up). Use this to resolve same-column clashes manually after adding events. Mid-bar anchoring means no special correction is needed for short or single-month bars.

Mandatory procedure for adding events to _data/career.yml

Follow these steps in order every time a new event is added. Do not skip steps.

Step 1 — Add the entry to career.yml Add the new event with correct start_year, start_month, end_year, end_month (or present: true), category, label, description, and leader_y_offset: 0.

Step 2 — Compute the label y-position for the new event and check for clashes and crowding

The label anchor y for a given event:

bar_top (non-present) = ((display_end_year − end_year) × 12 − end_month + 1) × 80 / 12 + 20
bar_top (present)     = 20
bar_mid               = bar_h / 2   where bar_h = duration_months × 80 / 12
leader_y              = bar_top + bar_mid + leader_y_offset   (default anchor: midpoint)
leader_y              = bar_top + leader_y_offset              (if leader_anchor: top)

(display_end_year = current year + 1, svg_top_pad = 20, px_per_year = 80)

Two labels conflict if they share the same label column and their leader_y values are within 32px of each other. Apply this check even when events are in different columns if their labels are long enough to visually overlap horizontally.

  • Left column: Education + Research
  • Right column: Work + Amateur Interest

Step 3 — Resolve conflicts with leader_y_offset If two labels in the same column are within 32px of each other, adjust leader_y_offset on the lower event (positive = push down) until the gap is ≥ 32px. Never use a negative offset that would pull the anchor above bar_top.

If two labels are in different columns but the same horizontal band (within 28px of each other) and one label is long enough to bleed into the other column, also push the lower one down.

Step 4 — Verify the leader line stays within the bar span The anchor must always fall within [bar_top, bar_top + bar_h]. After setting leader_y_offset, check:

anchor_y = bar_top + bar_mid + leader_y_offset        (default)
anchor_y = bar_top + leader_y_offset                  (leader_anchor: top)

Must satisfy: bar_top ≤ anchor_y ≤ bar_top + bar_h

The maximum safe positive offset from midpoint = bar_h - bar_mid = bar_h / 2. The maximum safe negative offset from midpoint = -bar_mid = -(bar_h / 2).

If resolving a clash forces leader_y_offset beyond these bounds — meaning the two bars are so close there is no room to separate the labels while keeping both anchors on their bars — prefer pushing the lower label below the lower bar’s bottom (anchor_y > bar_top + bar_h is acceptable as a last resort) rather than letting labels overlap. Document this exception with a comment in career.yml.

Workflow

This project follows the edward-dev-workflow via .dev-manager/state.json.

Current state: DEVELOPING — career timeline month-precision implemented.
